response:
To find the **characteristic polynomial** of a matrix $ A $, we compute the determinant of $ A - \lambda I $, where $ I $ is the identity matrix of the same size as $ A $.

Given matrix:

$$
A = \begin{pmatrix}
-4 & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14
\end{pmatrix}
$$

We compute the characteristic polynomial $ p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I) $.

---

### Step 1: Compute $ A - \lambda I $

$$
A - \lambda I = \begin{pmatrix}
-4 - \lambda & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 - \lambda & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{pmatrix}
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the determinant

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = \begin{vmatrix}
-4 - \lambda & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 - \lambda & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
$$

We expand this determinant using cofactor expansion or a computational method.

---

### Step 3: Expand the determinant

Let’s compute the determinant:

$$
\begin{vmatrix}
-4 - \lambda & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 - \lambda & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
$$

We use cofactor expansion along the first row:

$$
(-4 - \lambda) \begin{vmatrix}
10 - \lambda & 4 \\
-5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
- (-13) \begin{vmatrix}
-9 & 4 \\
12 & -14 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
+ 10 \begin{vmatrix}
-9 & 10 - \lambda \\
12 & -5
\end{vmatrix}
$$

---

10 - \lambda & 4 \\
-5 & -14 - \lambda
